raw: {"weights": {"R1.1": 0.7, "R1.2": 0.7, "R1.3": 0.3, "R1.4": 0.3, "R1.5": 0.3, "R1.6": 0.7, "R1.7": 0.0, "RX.1": 1.0, "RX.2": 1.0, "RX.3": 1.0, "RX.4": 1.0, "RX.5": 1.0}, "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"], "rationale": {"R1.1": "The artifact contains an explicit contribution claim ('unified evaluation framework', 'deployment-gap metric', 'commitment reliability frontier'); evaluating whether the thesis and gap statement are clearly and accurately articulated is central to any meaningful review of this section.", "R1.2": "The section mixes formal constraints, a two-level architecture, an asymmetric settlement model, and a complexity-reduction argument; checking that premises, intermediate steps, and conclusions cohere without internal contradiction is a core review responsibility.", "R1.6": "A section review canonically includes assessing sentence and paragraph quality; several passages here are dense or underspecified (e.g., 'weights chosen either fixed a priori or learned during training' with no further elaboration), making writing polish a central expected output."}, "clarification_needed": true, "clarification_question": "Should this review focus primarily on technical and logical correctness of the formalization (making R1.2 load-bearing at 1.0), or primarily on writing and presentation quality (making R1.6 load-bearing at 1.0), rather than treating both as equally central?"}

rationale.R1.1: The artifact contains an explicit contribution claim ('unified evaluation framework', 'deployment-gap metric', 'commitment reliability frontier'); evaluating whether the thesis and gap statement are clearly and accurately articulated is central to any meaningful review of this section.
rationale.R1.2: The section mixes formal constraints, a two-level architecture, an asymmetric settlement model, and a complexity-reduction argument; checking that premises, intermediate steps, and conclusions cohere without internal contradiction is a core review responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: A section review canonically includes assessing sentence and paragraph quality; several passages here are dense or underspecified (e.g., 'weights chosen either fixed a priori or learned during training' with no further elaboration), making writing polish a central expected output.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review focus primarily on technical and logical correctness of the formalization (making R1.2 load-bearing at 1.0), or primarily on writing and presentation quality (making R1.6 load-bearing at 1.0), rather than treating both as equally central?
